reasoning: The Paris prosecutor has confirmed that multiple suspects have been arrested and formally charged over the 2025 Louvre robbery. Crucially, the authorities stated that three of the four members of the "commando" team that physically carried out the theft in the museum are currently in custody. Because these individuals were part of the actual physical robbery, they meet the resolution criterion for perpetrators directly involved in the robbery. Suspects were apprehended while attempting to flee the country and have reportedly "partially admitted" their involvement, making it highly unlikely they will be completely exonerated by a "consensus of credible reporting" within the 1-month wait period set by the market creator. With arrests made in late October, the conditions for a YES resolution are solidly fulfilled, provided no sudden exoneration occurs by late November. The market itself was already at 95% shortly after the initial arrests, and subsequent news confirms their role and limits the chance of exoneration.
